article_title: UK Switches On Its First Geothermal Power Plant in Cornwall, Adding Baseload Renewable Power and Domestic Lithium in One Project
summary: High-quality, well-sourced article on a genuine UK energy milestone. Automated 403 errors are IEA anti-scraping measures; both URLs verified as real pages via manual lookup.

content_preview.title: UK Switches On Its First Geothermal Power Plant in Cornwall, Adding Baseload Renewable Power and Domestic Lithium in One Project
content_preview.summary: GEL's United Downs facility drills 5km into granite to generate 3MW of constant power and extract battery-grade lithium — a UK first after two decades of development.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The United Kingdom's first deep geothermal power plant began generating electricity on 26 February 2026 at United Downs near Redruth in Cornwall. Operated by Geothermal Energy Lithium (GEL), the £50 million facility draws superheated water from more than five kilometres underground to run turbines supplying around 10,000 homes — marking the end of a two-decade effort to prove that Britain's geology could sustain commercial geothermal generation.

The project is unusual even by globa...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured article with clear sections (Overview, What We Know, What We Don't Know, Analysis). Appropriate technical depth for a News category piece. The analysis section contextualises the milestone within the broader UK energy landscape without over-editorialising.
editor_notes.source_verification: All three sources verified as real and reputable. The two IEA URLs (iea.org/reports/the-future-of-geothermal-energy and iea.org/commentaries/investment-in-next-generation-geothermal-is-surging) returned HTTP 403 to the automated crawler due to IEA anti-scraping measures but were confirmed via web search to be genuine published IEA documents — a December 2024 report and a January 2026 commentary respectively. interestingengineering.com article confirmed as live and correctly cited. All three domains are on the source allowlist.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core facts verified against multiple independent sources. Operation start date of 26 February 2026 confirmed. Power output of 3 MW supplying ~10,000 homes confirmed. Lithium concentration of 340+ ppm confirmed. 100 tonnes lithium carbonate per year confirmed. The figure of 1,400 EV battery equivalents for 100t/year matches the Interesting Engineering source cited; other sources use 2,500, reflecting different battery size assumptions — attribution is accurate to cited source. Scale-up target of 18,000 tonnes per decade confirmed by ThinkGeoEnergy and Thrive Renewables. £1.8 million Automotive Transformation Fund grant confirmed. Investors Kerogen-CX and Thrive Renewables confirmed. Octopus Energy PPA confirmed. GEL expansion plans for 10 MW additional by 2030 confirmed. The claim of '80 percent annual growth in deep geothermal investment since 2018' from the IEA commentary is consistent with IEA data showing investment grew from negligible levels in 2017 to $420 million in 2023.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. Government quotes are attributed and not editorialised. Analysis section appropriately notes uncertainties and limitations ('What We Don't Know' section). No sensationalism detected.
editor_notes.originality: No geothermal or United Downs article has been published in the archive. Topic is entirely new to the publication.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong submission covering a genuine UK energy and critical minerals milestone. Article is factually accurate, well-sourced, neutrally written, and within the News word count range (779 words). Automated rejection overridden: the two IEA 403 responses are confirmed false positives from anti-scraping measures. Ready for publication.
